rationale.R1.1: The section asserts a gap (aggregated benchmarks conflate regimes) and a contribution claim (NUMSKILL disentangles competences into a skill-resolved profile); verifying that framing is coherent and justified is central to any meaningful review of this text.
rationale.R1.2: The protocol chain—142 curated IVPs → fixed-T global error → log-error headline metric → bootstrap bounds → solver rankings—contains internal dependencies; logical consistency review must confirm each step follows from the prior and that the bootstrap is applied correctly to the reported quantity.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete empirical claims are made (Radau IIA E=-1.21 at TOL=1e-9; DOPRI5 E=+0.34 at TOL=1e-3; wall-clock normalized to DOPRI5) and attributed to Table 2; alignment between those claims and what a correctly constructed table would show is directly in scope.
rationale.R1.4: The section closes with an explicit priority claim ('first fine-grained, skill-resolved profile of widely deployed numerical ODE solvers'); validating that claim against known literature (DETEST variants, Hairer–Wanner testbanks, recent solver benchmarking papers) is a central review obligation given how prominently the claim is positioned.

parsed.clarification_question: Is the intended review primarily technical/scientific—checking correctness of the methodology, internal logical consistency, and validity of the novelty claim—or primarily editorial/writing-focused, checking prose clarity, paragraph structure, and sentence quality?
